label: Kyiv
sub: Capital of Ukraine; survived Russian siege Feb-March 2022

label: Kharkiv
sub: Ukraine's 2nd largest city; survived multiple Russian offensives; counteroffensive liberated 8,000 sq km (Sept 2022)

label: Mariupol
sub: Besieged and destroyed by Russia (Feb-May 2022); Azovstal defenders surrendered May 16-20, 2022

label: Simferopol (Crimea)
sub: Capital of Crimea; illegally annexed by Russia March 2014; internationally recognized as Ukrainian territory

label: Donetsk
sub: Occupied by Russia-backed forces since April 2014; illegally annexed September 2022; Ukrainian government in exile in Kramatorsk

label: Zaporizhzhia
sub: Frontline city; oblast illegally annexed by Russia (Sept 2022) but city remains under Ukrainian control; near ZNPP

label: Kherson
sub: Liberated by Ukraine November 11, 2022; first occupied regional capital retaken; under constant Russian shelling from left bank

label: Chernobyl
sub: Site of 1986 nuclear disaster; Russian forces occupied briefly Feb-March 2022; 30km exclusion zone remains

label: Lviv
sub: Cultural capital of western Ukraine; major transit hub for Western military aid; historically part of Habsburg Austria and interwar Poland

label: Odesa
sub: Major Black Sea port; survived Russian naval bombardment; key to Ukrainian grain exports under the Black Sea Grain Initiative

label: Bakhmut
sub: Site of the longest and bloodiest battle of the war (July 2022 – May 2023); captured by Wagner Group; city largely destroyed

label: Avdiivka
sub: Strategically fortified Ukrainian stronghold; held since 2014; fell to Russia February 17, 2024

label: Bucha
sub: Site of mass atrocities by Russian forces; 400+ civilians found killed after Russian withdrawal March 2022; ICC war crimes investigation

label: Hostomel Airport
sub: Site of failed Russian VDV assault Feb 24, 2022; Russian paratroopers repelled in defense of Kyiv's northern approach

label: Irpin
sub: Kyiv suburb; evacuations under fire became iconic images of early war; liberated by Ukraine March 28, 2022

label: Babi Yar
sub: Site of Holocaust massacre: 33,771 Jews killed Sept 29-30, 1941 by Nazi SS Einsatzgruppen; now a memorial site in Kyiv

label: Kerch Bridge
sub: Bridge connecting Russia to occupied Crimea; completed 2018; partially destroyed Oct 8, 2022 and July 2023 in Ukrainian attacks

label: Zaporizhzhia NPP
sub: Europe's largest nuclear power plant; seized by Russia March 4, 2022; used as military base; IAEA monitoring since Sept 2022

label: Kramatorsk
sub: Administrative center of Ukrainian-held Donetsk Oblast; Kramatorsk railway station strike killed 59 civilians April 8, 2022

label: Izium
sub: Key Russian logistics hub; liberated by Ukraine in Kharkiv counteroffensive (Sept 2022); mass graves with evidence of torture found

label: Poltava
sub: Site of the 1709 Battle of Poltava where Peter the Great defeated Hetman Mazepa and Swedish King Charles XII, ending the Cossack bid for independence

label: Kakhovka Dam
sub: Site of catastrophic dam destruction June 6, 2023; flooding killed dozens and caused major ecological damage to the Dnipro delta

label: Melitopol
sub: Occupied by Russia since March 2022; key logistics hub and air base; Ukrainian counteroffensive (2023) targeted Melitopol corridor

label: Berdyansk
sub: Occupied Black Sea port; Ukraine sank Russian landing ship Saratov here March 24, 2022 using TB-2 drone

label: Dnipro
sub: Ukraine's third largest city; key logistics and industrial hub; site of Russian missile strikes on apartment buildings

label: Mykolaiv
sub: Southern city that repelled Russian advance on Odesa; key to Ukraine holding its Black Sea coastline

label: Chernihiv
sub: Northern city besieged by Russian forces Feb-March 2022; cut off from Kyiv supply lines; liberated after Russian withdrawal

label: Sumy
sub: Northeastern city on Russian border; endured sustained cross-border shelling; launch point for Ukrainian Kursk incursion (Aug 2024)

label: Lyman
sub: Strategic rail hub liberated by Ukraine October 1, 2022 during Kharkiv counteroffensive, the same day Putin announced illegal annexation

label: Khortytsia Island
sub: Island near Zaporizhzhia; historic center of the Zaporozhian Sich Cossack Host; symbol of Ukrainian Cossack identity

label: Pereiaslav
sub: Site of the 1654 Pereiaslav Agreement between Hetman Khmelnytsky and Muscovy Tsar Alexis — the most contested treaty in Ukrainian history

label: Vuhledar
sub: Strategic coal town on southern Donetsk front; Ukrainian defenders destroyed hundreds of Russian armored vehicles in 2022-23

label: Soledar
sub: Salt mine town captured by Wagner Group January 2023; fighting over vast underground mine tunnels used as shelter

label: Uzhhorod
sub: Western gateway city on EU border; major transit point for refugees and Western military aid entering Ukraine

label: Sevastopol
sub: Russian Black Sea Fleet base in occupied Crimea; home port extended to 2042 under Kharkiv Accords (2010); targeted by Ukrainian naval drones in 2023-24
